children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Not later than April 1 of each year, the Federal Maritime Commission shall submit a report to Congress. The report shall include the results of its investigations, a summary of its transactions, the purposes for which all of its expenditures were made, and any recommendations for legislation.

children.heading: .—Report on Foreign Laws and Practices
children.label: b .—Report on Foreign Laws and Practices

children.text: a list of the 20 foreign countries that generated the largest volume of oceanborne liner cargo for the most recent calendar year in bilateral trade with the United States;
an analysis of conditions described in being investigated or found to exist in foreign countries;section 42302(a) of this title
any actions being taken by the Commission to offset those conditions;
any recommendations for additional legislation to offset those conditions;
a list of petitions filed under that the Commission rejected and the reasons for each rejection;section 42302(b) of this title

an analysis of the impacts on competition for the purchase of certain covered services by alliances of ocean common carriers acting pursuant to an agreement under this part between or among ocean common carriers, including a summary of actions, including corrective actions, taken by the Commission to promote such competition; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: identified as a nonmarket economy country (as defined in section 771(18) of the Tariff Act of 1930 ()) as of the date of enactment of this paragraph;19 U.S.C. 1677(18)

children.label: ii
children.text: identified by the United States Trade Representative in the most recent report required by section 182 of the Trade Act of 1974 () as a priority foreign country under subsection (a)(2) of that section; or19 U.S.C. 2242

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to monitoring by the United States Trade Representative under section 306 of the Trade Act of 1974 ().19 U.S.C. 2416

children.heading: .—Definition of Certain Covered Services
children.label: c .—Definition of Certain Covered Services
children.text: In this section, the term “certain covered services” has the meaning given the term in section 40102.
all findings by the Commission of false detention and demurrage invoice information by common carriers under ; andsection 41104(a)(15) of this title
all penalties imposed or assessed against common carriers, as applicable, under sections 41107, 41108, and 41109, listed by each common carrier.
